note: The equality BAC=CDB follows from the triangle congruence (Prop.26, arg_i 5), not from adding equals. CN.2 is irrelevant here.
This is a statement/heading ('a diagonal cuts them in half'), not a reasoning step. It should not cite CN.1; CN.1 is not invoked to make this claim. It sets up for the next steps.
This step just restates: AB=CD (given), BC common. Neither Prop.29 nor Prop.26 is invoked; this is setup for Prop.4 to be applied in the next step.
Prop.34 gives AD=EF (arg_i 2, 3, 4). Then CN.2 adds common segment DE to conclude AE=DF. The cited Prop.34 is the source of the initial equalities, but the conclusion step uses CN.2 (adding equals).
This step restates the two equalities from arg_i 6 & 7 (AE=FD, AB=DC). Prop.29 (alternate angles) is not invoked. This is just assembly of facts for Prop.4.
Prop.34 states that a diagonal bisects a parallelogram. DC bisects DBCF, so triangle DBC is half. CN.3 (subtracting equals) is not needed; the bisection is direct from Prop.34.
Triangle FED is half of parallelogram DEFH because the diagonal DF bisects it (Prop.34). CN.3 is not invoked; the bisection is a direct conclusion from Prop.34.
